title: Xautoを呼び出して
summary_text: Safest option is: **keep `xauto-prod-verify-gha` untouched**, and publish from a **new clean branch off `origin/main`** in a **separate worktree**, then cherry-pick only the intended commits and open a PR. Reasoning: your current worktree is heavily dirty, so any in-place cleanup (`stash`, branch switching, reset/rebase) increases contamination and recovery risk. A clean worktree + cherry-pick is non-destructive, auditable, and minimizes accidental scope drift. Minimal safe flow: ```bash git -C /Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public fetch origin git -C /Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public worktree add /tmp/fugue-pub origin/main cd /tmp/fugue-pub git switch -c pub/xauto-prod-verify git cherry-pick <relevant-commit-sha>... git push -u origin pub/xauto-prod-verify ``` If scope must stay strictly xauto-prod-verify, cherry-pick only those commits and leave broader hardening commits out of this PR.
